      "それはオランダ北部のフリースラント州で開催されました。"

      "それは1909年に22人の男性によって行われました。",

About: It is about a long-distance tour skating event on natural ice.
Affects: It affects the province of Friesland in the Netherlands.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the Association of the Eleven Frisian Towns.
BelongsTo: It belongs to the tradition of Frisian skating.
CausedBy: It is caused by climate change.
ConsistsOf: It consists of a route leading past all eleven historical cities of Friesland.
HasA: It has a starting permit and bib requirement.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by being held only when the natural ice is at least 15 centimeters thick.
HeldIn: It was held in the province of Friesland in the north of the Netherlands.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by the tradition of skaters visiting all eleven cities of Friesland on one day since 1760.
IsA: It is a long-distance tour skating event.
It is the biggest ice-skating tour in the world.
It is a speed skating competition.
KnownAs: It is known as the Eleven cities tour.
KnownFor: It is known for its rarity due to ice conditions.
It is known for the excitement it creates when declared.
It is known for the "Elfstedenkoorts" or eleven cities fever.
LocatedIn: It is located in the province of Friesland in the north of the Netherlands.
OccurredDuring: It occurred during the winters of 1939/40, 1940/41 and 1941/42.
It occurred during a ten-day cold spell in late January and early February 2012.
PartOf: It is part of Frisian tradition.
PerformedBy: It was performed by 300 contestants in the speed skating competition.
It was performed by 16,000 skaters in the leisure tour.
It was performed by 22 men in 1909.
It was performed by Jikke Gaastra in 1912.
It was performed by Janna van der Weg in 1917.
It was performed by over 3,000 competitors in 1940.
It was performed by 69 of the 10,000 participants in 1963.
It was performed by Crown Prince Willem-Alexander in 1986.
It was performed by Piet Kleine in 1997.
UsedFor: It is used for a speed skating competition.
It is used for a leisure tour.
WonBy: It was won by Reinier Paping in 1963.
It was won by Klasina Seinstra in 1997.
